author: The Seventh Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Nice
book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Seventh Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Nice.
The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.

metadata.author: The Seventh Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Nice

title: Canon VI.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: Concerning the holding of a local Synod at the time appointed.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: Since there is a canon which says, twice a year in each province, the canonical enquiries shall be made in the gatherings of the bishops; but because of the inconveniences which those who thus came together had to undergo in travelling, the holy fathers of the Sixth Council decreed that once each year, without regard to place or excuse which might be urged, a council should be held and the things which are amiss corrected.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: And if any prince be found hindering this being carried out, let him be excommunicated.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: But if any of the metropolitans shall take no care that this be done, he being free from constraint or fear or other reasonable excuse, let him be subjected to the canonical penalties.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: While the council is engaged in considering the canons or matters which have regard to the Gospel, it behoves the assembled Bishops, with all attention and grave thought to guard the divine and life-giving commandments of God, for in keeping of them there is great reward; because our lamp is the commandment, and our light is the law, and trial and discipline are the way of life, and the commandment of the Lord shining afar giveth light to the eyes.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: It is not permitted to a metropolitan to demand any of those things which the bishops bring with them, whether it be a horse or any other gift.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: If he be convicted of doing anything of this sort, he shall restore fourfold.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: Whenever it is not possible for a synod to meet according to the decree formulated long ago, twice in each year, at least let it be held once, as seemed good to the Sixth Synod.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: Should any magistrate forbid such meeting, let him be cast out: and a bishop who shall take no pains to assemble it, shall be subject to punishment.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: And when the synod is held, should it appear that the Metropolitan has taken anything away from any bishop, let him restore four-fold.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: Anastasius remarks on this, that this ordinance (whether the whole canon or only its last passage must remain undecided) was not accepted by the Latins.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: That this canon did not forbid the so-called Synodicum, which the metropolitans had lawfully to receive from the bishops, and the bishops from the priests, is remarked by Van Espen, l. c. p.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: Compare with this (as Balsamon advises) the eighth canon of the Council in Trullo.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: This canon is found in the Corpus Juris Canonici, Gratian’s Decretum, Pars I., Dist.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
